mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month growth in industrial production signals expanding manufacturing output and corporate earnings, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Month-over-month changes in INDPRO fail to show a positive rank correlation (Rank IC < 0.03) with 20-day SPX forward returns over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: An unemployment rate above 6% indicates substantial labor market slack that suppresses wage growth and consumer spending, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over the following quarter.
failure_condition: When UNRATE exceeds 6.0%, S&P 500 60-day forward returns are positive in more than 50% of triggered instances over a 3-year walk-forward window.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month increase in the federal funds rate concurrent with a month-over-month decrease in the consumer price index defines a real monetary tightening episode that raises real discount rates and produces negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint condition of rising FEDFUNDS and falling CPIAUCSL does not produce negative 20-day SPX returns in at least 55% of occurrences during walk-forward testing.

mechanism_narrative: During post-crisis recovery, month-over-month increases in retail sales signal accelerating private demand that supports corporate revenue growth and generates positive S&P 500 returns over the next week.
failure_condition: RSAFS month-over-month increases during post-crisis recovery periods fail to predict positive 5-day SPX returns with Rank IC > 0.04 over any 2-year window.

mechanism_narrative: Consecutive monthly declines in nonfarm payrolls signal deteriorating household income and aggregate demand, triggering risk-off repositioning and negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Two-consecutive-month declines in PAYEMS are followed by negative 5-day SPX returns in less than 55% of triggered observations over a 2-year window.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous month-over-month declines in housing starts and increases in the unemployment rate capture a joint deterioration in construction-sensitive and labor-income demand, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: The joint signal of falling HOUST and rising UNRATE does not underperform the unconditional SPX baseline by at least 2% annualized over a 3-year walk-forward window.

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month contraction in industrial production below zero growth signals supply-side earnings compression and inventory destocking, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over the following quarter.
failure_condition: Months with INDPRO month-over-month change below zero are not associated with negative 60-day SPX returns in at least 60% of cases.

mechanism_narrative: During monetary tightening cycles, a month-over-month increase in the federal funds rate combined with a month-over-month decline in retail sales identifies a policy mistake where tightening suppresses demand, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint occurrence of rising FEDFUNDS and falling RSAFS during tightening cycles fails to produce negative 20-day SPX returns in at least 55% of instances.
